items.title: Taylor uses own televised address to criticise PM’s prime-time speech - video
items.standfirst: Federal opposition leader Angus Taylor criticises the prime minister’s national address made on Wednesday night and says it failed to provide clarity or detail on the fuel crisis caused by the war in Iran. Taylor accuses the government of fuelling confusion and not acting with urgency, adding Australians 'received neither' answers nor detail Continue reading...
items.content: Federal opposition leader Angus Taylor criticises the prime minister’s national address made on Wednesday night and says it failed to provide clarity or detail on the fuel crisis caused by the war in Iran. Taylor accuses the government of fuelling confusion and not acting with urgency, adding Australians 'received neither' answers nor detail Continue reading...

items.title: Angus Taylor says 'put Australia first' during national address
items.standfirst: Angus Taylor has issued a return serve to the prime minister's national address by urging people to "put Australia first" while accusing the government of providing a lack of clarity about the ongoing fuel crisis.
items.content: Angus Taylor has issued a return serve to the prime minister's national address by urging people to "put Australia first" while accusing the government of providing a lack of clarity about the ongoing fuel crisis.

items.title: ‘Dig and drill’: Angus Taylor says Australia should fast-track mining and coal projects amid fuel crisis
items.standfirst: Opposition leader uses televised address to suggest Anthony Albanese’s televised address could have been a social media postGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastAngus Taylor says the global energy shock is proof that Australia should fast-track mining exploration and coal and gas project construction, as part of efforts to shore up fuel supply for future crises.Addressing Australians in a television broadcast on Thursday night, the opposition leader accused Labor of mishandling the disruption caused by the war in Iran and challenged Anthony Albanese to explain plans for emergency supply of petrol and diesel. Continue reading...
items.content: Opposition leader uses televised address to suggest Anthony Albanese’s televised address could have been a social media postGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastAngus Taylor says the global energy shock is proof that Australia should fast-track mining exploration and coal and gas project construction, as part of efforts to shore up fuel supply for future crises.Addressing Australians in a television broadcast on Thursday night, the opposition leader accused Labor of mishandling the disruption caused by the war in Iran and challenged Anthony Albanese to explain plans for emergency supply of petrol and diesel. Continue reading...

items.title: Anthony Albanese defends address to nation as bid to counter misinformation – video
items.standfirst: Australian prime minister, Anthony Albanese, defends his rare national address on Wednesday evening, delivered amid rising fuel supply concerns linked to the Middle East conflict. He says the address aimed to reassure Australians and counter growing misinformation, as social media disrupts access to consistent and reliable informationKeep calm, but here comes the pain. Albanese’s address presents Australians with a mixed messageAnthony Albanese addresses the nation on the Middle East crisis – read the speech in full Continue reading...
items.content: Australian prime minister, Anthony Albanese, defends his rare national address on Wednesday evening, delivered amid rising fuel supply concerns linked to the Middle East conflict. He says the address aimed to reassure Australians and counter growing misinformation, as social media disrupts access to consistent and reliable informationKeep calm, but here comes the pain. Albanese’s address presents Australians with a mixed messageAnthony Albanese addresses the nation on the Middle East crisis – read the speech in full Continue reading...

items.title: Keep calm, but here comes the pain. Albanese’s address presents Australians with a mixed message
items.standfirst: The prime minister’s decision to speak to the country could undermine what he was trying to convey, that everything is in hand and there is no need to panicGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastWhen news that Anthony Albanese was planning an address to the nation spread around Canberra on Wednesday morning, memories of those deep, dark days during the worst of the pandemic came rushing back.Amid fuel shortages and growing nervousness, a prime-time television address from the country’s leader was bound to remind anxious households of Covid-19 lockdowns and the chaos of the global financial crisis. Continue reading...
items.content: The prime minister’s decision to speak to the country could undermine what he was trying to convey, that everything is in hand and there is no need to panicGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastWhen news that Anthony Albanese was planning an address to the nation spread around Canberra on Wednesday morning, memories of those deep, dark days during the worst of the pandemic came rushing back.Amid fuel shortages and growing nervousness, a prime-time television address from the country’s leader was bound to remind anxious households of Covid-19 lockdowns and the chaos of the global financial crisis. clusters.title: One Nation wins fourth seat in SA – as it happened
clusters.neutral_headline: One Nation wins fourth seat in SA – as it happened
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian Australia, ABC News Australia

items.title: One Nation wins fourth seat in SA – as it happened
items.standfirst: Follow live updatesTrack Australia’s fuel prices, service station outages and shipments in chartsGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcast‘Might as well have told us what he was going to have for dinner’: shadow minister lambasts address to the nationThe quips continue coming in thick and fast against Albanese’s address to the nation. The shadow minister for energy, Dan Tehan, tells ABC Radio:He might as well have told us what he was going to have for dinner last night. There was nothing new in it. He didn’t take the Australian people into his confidence.He made no commitments to transparency … there was no commitment from the prime minister to tell us whether ships have been cancelled, whether they’re being delayed, what our stock holdings are at the moment, where the shortages are, how many service stations are out of fuel, what they’re doing to make sure they’re getting fuel to those service stations – nothing.We will be participating in that. It’ll be a virtual meeting as I understand the next 24 hours and the foreign minister will be representing Australia at that meeting.It follows on from Australia signing up to the UK-led statement … all of those countries and very much Australia have an interest in seeing the straits of Hormuz opened as soon as possible. We will look to what Australia can do. Continue reading...
items.content: Follow live updatesTrack Australia’s fuel prices, service station outages and shipments in chartsGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcast‘Might as well have told us what he was going to have for dinner’: shadow minister lambasts address to the nationThe quips continue coming in thick and fast against Albanese’s address to the nation. The shadow minister for energy, Dan Tehan, tells ABC Radio:He might as well have told us what he was going to have for dinner last night. There was nothing new in it. He didn’t take the Australian people into his confidence.He made no commitments to transparency … there was no commitment from the prime minister to tell us whether ships have been cancelled, whether they’re being delayed, what our stock holdings are at the moment, where the shortages are, how many service stations are out of fuel, what they’re doing to make sure they’re getting fuel to those service stations – nothing.We will be participating in that. It’ll be a virtual meeting as I understand the next 24 hours and the foreign minister will be representing Australia at that meeting.It follows on from Australia signing up to the UK-led statement … all of those countries and very much Australia have an interest in seeing the straits of Hormuz opened as soon as possible. We will look to what Australia can do. Continue reading...

items.title: Penny Wong to join talks with 35 countries, excluding US, to explore ways to reopen strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: Talks, convened by the UK, will examine ‘all viable diplomatic and political measures’ to get critical waterway openFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastPenny Wong will represent Australia at an international summit on efforts to reopen the strait of Hormuz, with UK prime minister, Keir Starmer, promising to explore “all viable diplomatic and political measures” to end the blockade of oil shipments.Starmer will host the talks, set to be attended by 35 nations, from about 10pm Thursday night, AEDT. Continue reading...
items.content: Talks, convened by the UK, will examine ‘all viable diplomatic and political measures’ to get critical waterway openFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastPenny Wong will represent Australia at an international summit on efforts to reopen the strait of Hormuz, with UK prime minister, Keir Starmer, promising to explore “all viable diplomatic and political measures” to end the blockade of oil shipments.Starmer will host the talks, set to be attended by 35 nations, from about 10pm Thursday night, AEDT. Continue reading...

items.title: Albanese announces crackdown on gambling ads, but falls well short of Labor’s own calls for total ban
items.standfirst: Albanese calls gambling reform ‘most significant’ Australia has seen but steps back from implementing all 31 recommendations of landmark 2023 reportFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe government will limit gambling advertising in what it has called the “most significant reform on gambling” Australia has ever seen, but has fallen short of key recommendations from a Labor-led report handed down more than 1,000 days ago.Gambling advertising will be banned in sports venues and capped in TV and radio broadcasts in a series of measures announced by the prime minister at the National Press Club on Thursday that advocates have warned do not go far enough.Capped to three ads an hour on broadcast television between 6am and 8:30pm;Banned on radio during school drop off and pick-up times;Banned from online platforms unless a user over 18 has logged on to an account; users will still have the option to opt-out of the advertising.Ban celebrities and sports players from appearing in gambling adds and odds-style ads.Ban gambling ads from sports venues and on players’ and officials’ uniforms. Continue reading...
items.content: Albanese calls gambling reform ‘most significant’ Australia has seen but steps back from implementing all 31 recommendations of landmark 2023 reportFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe government will limit gambling advertising in what it has called the “most significant reform on gambling” Australia has ever seen, but has fallen short of key recommendations from a Labor-led report handed down more than 1,000 days ago.Gambling advertising will be banned in sports venues and capped in TV and radio broadcasts in a series of measures announced by the prime minister at the National Press Club on Thursday that advocates have warned do not go far enough.Capped to three ads an hour on broadcast television between 6am and 8:30pm;Banned on radio during school drop off and pick-up times;Banned from online platforms unless a user over 18 has logged on to an account; users will still have the option to opt-out of the advertising.Ban celebrities and sports players from appearing in gambling adds and odds-style ads.Ban gambling ads from sports venues and on players’ and officials’ uniforms. Continue reading...

items.title: Albanese announces new restrictions on gambling advertising – video
items.standfirst: In a National Press Club address the Australian prime minister, Anthony Albanese, announces tougher restrictions on gambling advertising, building on earlier reforms aimed at reducing harm. New measures include capping TV ads, banning radio ads during school pickup and drop-off times, and restricting online ads to verified adults with opt-out options Continue reading...
items.content: In a National Press Club address the Australian prime minister, Anthony Albanese, announces tougher restrictions on gambling advertising, building on earlier reforms aimed at reducing harm. New measures include capping TV ads, banning radio ads during school pickup and drop-off times, and restricting online ads to verified adults with opt-out options Continue reading...

items.title: PM announces gambling ad ban in stadiums, during school pick-up
items.standfirst: Gambling ads to be partially restricted on television and radio, with opt-out rules for online promotions and a ban in stadiums and on jerseys under reforms unveiled by Labor.
items.content: Gambling ads to be partially restricted on television and radio, with opt-out rules for online promotions and a ban in stadiums and on jerseys under reforms unveiled by Labor.

items.standfirst: As detectives investigate whether the sovereign citizen community helped harbour police killer Dezi Freeman, researchers say the anti-government movement appears to be surging in regional Australia.
items.content: As detectives investigate whether the sovereign citizen community helped harbour police killer Dezi Freeman, researchers say the anti-government movement appears to be surging in regional Australia.

items.title: Museum fans: Test yourself with our curious collection of trivia
items.standfirst: Australia’s museums hold a treasure trove of odd facts, native animals and even burlesque history. So how much do you know about the things hidden in our nation’s vaults?
items.content: Australia’s museums hold a treasure trove of odd facts, native animals and even burlesque history. So how much do you know about the things hidden in our nation’s vaults?
